title: Arzid

{{Quote|Grand Moff Muzzer, you shall serve for five years as the lowly sentry at a small Imperial outpost on the planet Arzid, the planet of macaab mushrooms, tentacle-bushes, and deadly arachnors.|[[Supreme Prophet]] [[Rajah Ubooki|Kadann]]|<ref name="PotDS" />}}
'''Arzid''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was inhabited by [[Forest/Legends|forests]] of giant [[macaab mushroom]]s, [[tentacle-bush]]es, and by the dangerous [[arachnor]] that feasted on the mushrooms as part of their diet.<ref name="PotDS" />

It was also the site of a small [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] outpost. Death rates among sentries at this outpost, due to arachnors or tentacle bushes, were high enough that being sent there as a sentry was seen as a death sentence.<ref name="PotDS" />

In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], a squad of [[E4 baron droid]]s overthrew the administration of the only settlement on the planet, setting themselves up as warlords over its population.<ref name="NEGtD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>

==Description==
{{Quote|Watch out for arachnor webs—they're horribly sticky and rather huge. And keep your eyes to the ground, and beware of tentacle-bushes.|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="PotDS" />}}
Arzid was a hot, dense world, appearing gray from space.<ref name="PotDS" /> Located in the [[Arzid system/Legends|Arzid system]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> the world orbited the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Tiki-hava]], which was known for ejecting vast green [[fire storm]]s that cast an eerie space-glow for thousands of miles and could prove a challenge to navigating the system. Arzid was covered by [[Forest/Legends|forests]] of [[macaab mushroom]]s, which varied in size from small ground cover comparable to [[Flower/Legends|flowers]], to towering [[Fungus/Legends|fungi]] that were as big as [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. Other flora on the world included the [[tentacle-bush]], which evolved long, thin tentacles to capture small [[Arzid rodent|rodents]] that could also entangle the feet of any nearby [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]].<ref name="PotDS" />

Arzid was also the home of the [[arachnor]], a dangerous two-[[Meter/Legends|meter]] [[Spider/Legends|arachnid]] whose webbing was regarded as among the stickiest and most intractable of any arachnid [[Species/Legends|species]] in the galaxy. Macaab mushrooms were a primary component of the Arachnor diet, and chemicals in the mushrooms, when digested by an arachnor, were the cause of this stickiness. Though [[Sentience/Legends|nonsentient]], they have an instinctive understanding of how to use fear to direct prey into their webs.<ref name="EGAS" />

==History==
{{Quote|Perhaps you shall meet with better fortune than the last sentry, who stumbled into an arachnor web his first month on Arzid and was eaten by those hungry giant spiders.|Supreme Prophet Kadann|<ref name="PotDS" />}}
Arzid was located in an area of space that was largely unexplored until after [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]]. This region fell within the influence of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] at several points in history, including during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Following the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], Arzid was visited by [[Senior Anthropologist]] [[Mammon Hoole]] and his assistant, [[Biologist/Legends|biologist]] [[Chlar Kotchmin]]. Kotchmin became trapped in an arachnor web, and the arachnor had him completely encased in webbing within seconds. Hoole nearly became caught himself, but was able to confuse the creature enough to cause it to wander away. Hoole lacked the tools to free Kotchmin, who suffocated within the shroud of webbing before Hoole could return with an [[energy slicer]] from their [[Starship/Legends|ship]].<ref name="EGAS" />

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Arzid had become the location of a small Imperial [[outpost]]. The life expectancy of a [[Sentry droid/Legends|sentry]] at this outpost was so short that being sentenced to five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of sentry duty there was deemed a suitable punishment for [[Grand Moff]] [[Muzzer]], who had plead guilty to [[Treason/Legends|treason]] and disloyalty in a show trial held by the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]]. Muzzer was sent from [[Scardia Station]] under [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] guard to the outpost.<ref name="PotDS" />

At the same time, [[Senate Planetary Intelligence Network]] (SPIN) [[Medic/Legends|medical personnel]] determined that the only way to permanently disable an Imperial [[HoloNet transceiver implant|mind control implant]] in the tooth of [[Triclops]], a prisoner they were holding, was by using a chemical derived from macaab mushrooms. SPIN agents [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and [[Ken]] traveled by modified [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]] [[BTL Y-wing starfighter#BTL-S3 Courier variant|Y-wing]] to Arzid, the nearest source of the mushrooms to their [[Palace of the Woolamander|headquarters]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. There, Skywalker and Chewbacca were promptly ensnared by tentacle-bushes while Ken became stuck in an arachnor web. Skywalker was able to cut himself free with [[Luke Skywalker's green lightsaber/Legends|his lightsaber]] and rushed to assist Ken, only to himself be entangled by an arachnor. While he was able to kill the beast, his lightsaber slipped from his hand just as the sound of an [[LAVr QH-7 Chariot|Imperial command speeder]] heralded the arrival of Muzzer and his guards at the nearby sentry tower. The stormtroopers promptly investigated the commotion and discovered the ensnared Skywalker and Ken. They [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] the two, used a [[pulse-mass generator]] to melt the webbing away, and departed Arzid with the two prisoners. C-3PO and R2-D2 were subsequently able to free Chewbacca, and the trio pursued their abducted companions.<ref name="PotDS" /> Muzzer was subsequently executed by [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] [[Ysanne Isard]].<ref name="NEC" />

By [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], Arzid had developed a single settlement. The [[Administrator/Legends|administrators]] of this settlement were overthrown by a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Baktoid Combat Automata/Legends|Baktoid]] [[E4 baron droid]]s. These droids subsequently ruled the planet by force of arms.<ref name="NEGtD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Arzid first appeared in [[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']], the sixth and final book of the [[Adventures of a Jedi Prince|''Jedi Prince'']] series by [[Paul Davids|Paul]] and [[Hollace Davids]], published in 1993.<ref name="PotDS" />

==Appearances==
*[[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']] {{1st}}
